title: FNOL Submission and Claim Number Generation Backend
body:
## Summary
Implement Express.js endpoint for first notice of loss (FNOL) submission, claim validation, and unique claim number generation.

## Current Behaviour
No backend API exists for FNOL submission or claim creation.

## Proposed Solution
Create POST /api/claims endpoint accepting incident details, validate fields, generate unique claim number, and integrate with claims management system.

## Scope
- Create POST /api/claims endpoint accepting incident date, location, description, and policy number
- Validate incident date is in the past and within policy coverage period
- Generate unique claim number using timestamp and random alphanumeric suffix
- Integrate with claims management system API to create claim record
- Return claim number and submission confirmation to frontend

## Implementation
- Create server/routes/claims.js with POST / route
- Add JWT authentication middleware and extract user ID and policy number
- Validate request body fields using express-validator or Joi
- Generate claim number format: CLM-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX (timestamp + 5 random alphanumeric)
- Call claims management system API to create claim record with incident details
- Return { success: true, claimNumber, message: 'Claim submitted successfully' }

## Any Semantics
- Endpoint: POST /api/claims
- Authorization: Bearer JWT token in Authorization header
- Claim number format: CLM-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX
- Claims management system integration: REST API
- Validation rules: incidentDate <= today, incidentDate >= policy effectiveDate, incidentDescription length >= 20

## Json Output Shapes
- POST /api/claims request: { policyNumber: 'POL123456', incidentDate: '2024-12-15', incidentLocation: '401 Highway near Toronto', incidentDescription: 'Rear-ended at red light, moderate damage to bumper' }
- POST /api/claims response: { success: true, claimNumber: 'CLM-20241215-A3F9K', message: 'Claim submitted successfully', estimatedProcessingTime: '2-3 business days' }

## List of Affected Files
- server/routes/claims.js
- server/controllers/claimController.js
- server/services/claimService.js
- server/utils/claimNumberGenerator.js
- server/middleware/authMiddleware.js

## Acceptance Criteria
- POST /api/claims accepts FNOL submission and returns claim number
- Endpoint validates JWT token and associates claim with authenticated user
- Endpoint validates incident date is in the past and within policy coverage period
- Endpoint validates incident location is non-empty and incident description is at least 20 characters
- Unique claim number is generated and returned in response

## Test Plan
- Verify POST /api/claims accepts valid FNOL submission and returns claim number
- Verify endpoint returns 401 for missing or invalid JWT token
- Verify endpoint rejects incident date in the future with 400 error
- Verify endpoint rejects incident date before policy effective date with 400 error
- Verify endpoint rejects incident description shorter than 20 characters with 400 error
- Verify claim number format matches CLM-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX pattern

## Test Profile
Backend API validation, claim number generation, and claims system integration

## Technical Notes
- Use uuid or nanoid for random alphanumeric suffix in claim number to ensure uniqueness
- Store claim submission in database before calling external claims system to ensure no data loss
- Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for claims system API calls
- Add fraud detection flags if incident description contains suspicious keywords

## Related Stories
- File a Claim frontend form implementation
- Document upload integration for claim attachments
- Claims management system authentication and API integration
- Notification service for claim confirmation email to policyholder

## Mockup Alignment
- Mockup shows FNOL form with incident date, location, description fields, and document upload section
- Form layout matches expected backend request payload structure

## Associated Mockups
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/6e6610e384ef4f58804300f07e7bf582](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/3b83f488ab825ac9)
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/dbb1e885dabb4a5daa3cf1bc1edd903a](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/53a946daf6a5aa74)
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/43901c5d01af4181a6005897aab5eb8b](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/668f008e07ab47ba)

title: Policy Retrieval and Proof of Insurance Generation Backend
body:
## Summary
Implement Express.js endpoints for retrieving policy details and generating proof-of-insurance PDF documents.

## Current Behaviour
No backend API exists for policy retrieval or proof-of-insurance generation.

## Proposed Solution
Create GET /api/policies/:policyNumber and GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof endpoints, integrate with policy administration system, and generate PDF using PDFKit or Puppeteer.

## Scope
- Create GET /api/policies/:policyNumber endpoint returning policy details
- Validate JWT token and authorize policy access based on user session
- Integrate with core policy administration system via REST or SOAP
- Create GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof endpoint generating PDF
- Include policy number, coverage type, limits, deductibles, and effective/expiry dates in PDF

## Implementation
- Create server/routes/policies.js with GET /:policyNumber and GET /:policyNumber/proof routes
- Add JWT authentication middleware verifying token and extracting user ID
- Call policy administration system API using axios or soap client to fetch policy data
- Map policy response to standardized JSON schema for frontend consumption
- Use PDFKit library to generate proof-of-insurance PDF with policy data
- Set Content-Type: application/pdf and Content-Disposition: attachment headers for PDF download

## Any Semantics
- Endpoint: GET /api/policies/:policyNumber
- Authorization: Bearer JWT token in Authorization header
- Policy administration system integration: REST API or SOAP service
- PDF generation library: PDFKit or Puppeteer
- Response format: JSON for policy details, PDF Blob for proof document

## Json Output Shapes
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber response: { policyNumber: 'POL123456', coverageType: 'Comprehensive', coverageLimit: 1000000, deductible: 500, effectiveDate: '2024-01-01', expiryDate: '2025-01-01', vehicles: [{ make: 'Toyota', model: 'Camry', year: 2022, vin: '1HGCM82633A123456' }] }
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof response: PDF Blob with policy card layout

## List of Affected Files
- server/routes/policies.js
- server/controllers/policyController.js
- server/services/policyService.js
- server/services/pdfGenerator.js
- server/middleware/authMiddleware.js

## Acceptance Criteria
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber returns policy details JSON matching schema
- Endpoint validates JWT token and returns 401 if missing or invalid
- Endpoint returns 403 if user is not authorized to access requested policy
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof returns PDF file with correct policy information
- PDF includes Canadian insurance card format with bilingual English/French text

## Test Plan
- Verify GET /api/policies/:policyNumber returns correct policy details for authenticated user
- Verify endpoint returns 401 for missing or invalid JWT token
- Verify endpoint returns 403 if user requests policy they do not own
- Verify GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof returns valid PDF file
- Verify PDF contains correct policy number, coverage details, and bilingual text
- Verify policy data fetch from policy administration system succeeds

## Test Profile
Backend API integration, authorization logic, and PDF generation

## Technical Notes
- Cache policy data in Redis for 5 minutes to reduce load on policy administration system
- Implement rate limiting on policy endpoints to prevent abuse
- Use async/await for policy administration system API calls with timeout handling
- Generate PDF asynchronously if generation takes longer than 2 seconds

## Related Stories
- Dashboard frontend policy display
- Policy administration system integration authentication setup
- Audit logging for policy access requests
- Multi-policy support for policyholders with multiple vehicles

title: My Profile Screen with Personal Information Management
body:
## Summary
Build the My Profile screen allowing policyholders to view and update contact information, driver's license number, and password.

## Current Behaviour
No profile management screen exists for policyholders.

## Proposed Solution
Implement React profile page matching the My Profile mockup, with editable fields for address, phone, driver's license, and password change form.

## Scope
- Create /profile route and Profile component in client/src/pages/
- Fetch current user profile from GET /api/users/profile endpoint
- Render editable form fields for full name, address, phone number, and driver's license
- Add Save Changes button sending PUT /api/users/profile request
- Add Change Password section with current/new password fields

## Implementation
- Create Profile.jsx component with useEffect to fetch GET /api/users/profile on mount
- Use React useState to manage form field state (fullName, address, phoneNumber, driversLicenseNumber)
- Add onSubmit handler calling axios.put('/api/users/profile', updatedFields)
- Render ChangePassword.jsx component with current/new password fields
- Validate phone number format (Canadian formats), driver's license format per province rules
- Display toast notification or inline message on successful update

## Any Semantics
- Route path: /profile
- Protected route: Requires valid JWT token
- Profile data source: GET /api/users/profile
- Profile update endpoint: PUT /api/users/profile
- Password change endpoint: POST /api/auth/change-password

## Json Output Shapes
- GET /api/users/profile response: { fullName: 'John Doe', dateOfBirth: '1980-05-15', address: '123 Maple St, Toronto ON', phoneNumber: '+1-416-555-0100', driversLicenseNumber: 'D1234-56789-12345' }
- PUT /api/users/profile request: { address: '456 Oak St, Toronto ON', phoneNumber: '+1-416-555-0200', driversLicenseNumber: 'D1234-56789-12345' }
- PUT /api/users/profile response: { success: true, message: 'Profile updated successfully' }

## List of Affected Files
- client/src/pages/Profile.jsx
- client/src/components/ChangePassword.jsx
- client/src/utils/validation.js
- client/src/App.js (route configuration)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Profile screen renders user's current information in editable form fields
- Save Changes button updates profile via PUT /api/users/profile and displays success message
- Change Password form validates current password and new password strength
- Profile update fails gracefully with inline error messages if validation fails
- All form fields support keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements

## Test Plan
- Verify profile screen fetches and displays current user information
- Verify Save Changes button updates profile and displays success message
- Verify phone number validation rejects invalid Canadian phone formats
- Verify driver's license validation rejects invalid formats
- Verify Change Password form validates current password and enforces new password strength
- Verify keyboard navigation works for all form fields and buttons

## Test Profile
Frontend form validation, API integration, and accessibility compliance

## Technical Notes
- Use libphonenumber-js for Canadian phone number validation
- Implement provincial driver's license format validation (Ontario, Quebec, BC formats differ)
- Add WCAG AA compliant error messages with aria-describedby linking to field errors
- Consider adding profile photo upload in future iteration

## Related Stories
- User profile backend API implementation
- Password change backend validation and hashing
- Driver's license verification integration with provincial databases
- Address validation and autocomplete using Canada Post API

## Mockup Alignment
- Mockup shows profile form with sections for contact information and driver's license details
- Use form layout with labeled input fields and Save Changes button per mockup

## Associated Mockups
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/6e6610e384ef4f58804300f07e7bf582](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/3b83f488ab825ac9)
